summary: Conjecture 25 proposes the natural extension of the paper's main theorem (Theorem 1) to k-uniform hypergraphs: every finite k-uniform hypergraph H with both an edge and a non-edge admits a countably infinite k-uniform hypergraph G_H such that every locally finite perturbation of G_H has an induced copy of H. The paper itself proves the k=2 (graph) case as its central result; the conjecture is explicitly stated as an open problem for general k ≥ 3. The paper has appeared in the Canadian Journal of Mathematics (published March 2026), but no follow-up work resolving or partially resolving this hypergraph extension was found in any search.
search_queries: k-uniform hypergraph induced-saturated locally finite perturbation countably infinite Bonamy Groenland Johnston Morrison Scott 2025
induced-saturated k-uniform hypergraph infinite locally finite perturbation 2025 2026
notes: No follow-up found. The conjecture is the hypergraph generalisation of the paper's main theorem. The paper was posted June 2025 and published in the Canadian Journal of Mathematics (Cambridge University Press, March 2026); Conjecture 25 appears in the open problems section. The condition that H has both an edge and a non-edge is the k-uniform analogue of H being neither a clique nor an independent set.
